lead: A sharp, energy-driven jump in U.S. inflation led business news as war-related fuel costs pushed consumer prices up at the fastest pace in nearly four years. In technology, AI safety concerns, a security incident targeting OpenAI chief Sam Altman, renewed criticism of Xbox management, and fresh signs of Apple’s foldable iPhone progress underscored a sector balancing growth, risk, and product ambition.
summary_points: March inflation accelerated sharply as gasoline and diesel prices surged, raising fresh economic and political pressure on the White House.
A suspect was arrested after allegedly attacking Sam Altman’s home and threatening OpenAI headquarters, highlighting rising tensions around AI leaders.
Anthropic’s new Mythos model drew cybersecurity concern, reflecting broader unease about increasingly capable AI systems and their potential misuse.
Reports on Apple’s foldable iPhone testing and criticism from a former Bethesda executive kept focus on hardware strategy and platform leadership.
watchlist: Next U.S. inflation and fuel-price readings
